skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: A store manager reviewed the receipts from 60 customers selected at random from all the customers who made purchases on a busy Saturday. Of those selected, 18 receipts showed that the customer had purchased flowers. If 2,000 customers made purchases that Saturday, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

answerOptions.content: Exactly 200 customers must have purchased flowers on Saturday.
The best estimate for the number of customers who purchased flowers on Saturday is 600.
Exactly 600 customers must have purchased flowers on Saturday.
The best estimate for the number of customers who purchased flowers on Saturday is 200.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The sample proportion is 18/60 = 30%. Applying this to all 2,000 purchasing customers gives an estimate of 0.30 × 2,000 = 600. Because this comes from a sample, it is an estimate, not an exact count.

Choices A and C inappropriately use the word 'exactly.' Choice D uses an incorrect proportion.
